description: This schematic illustrates the evolution of inclusions during smelting and solidification of Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steels with three different Al contents (1 wt%, 3 wt%, 5 wt%). It shows the formation process of inclusions: primary single-phase inclusions including Al₂O₃, AlN, MnS, TiN precipitate first, then later-precipitated phases nucleate on earlier formed inclusions via heterogeneous nucleation to form composite inclusions such as Al₂O₃-MnS, AlN-MnS, TiN-MnS, and Al₂O₃-AlN-MnS. As Al content increases, the amount of single Al₂O₃ inclusions decreases while AlN inclusions increase, and the types of composite inclusions change accordingly: no Al₂O₃-AlN-MnS composite inclusions exist in the 5 wt% Al sample due to the low content of single Al₂O₃. This figure supports the conclusion that Al content modifies the solidification route, thus regulating the type, morphology, and composition of inclusions in the steels.
